ontology.foodex2_description: Cakes, pastries, sweet pies and desserts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code for Kueh Talam. A0170 (Cakes, pastries, sweet pies and desserts) is the closest parent category for this traditional steamed layered sweet cake.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for Kueh Talam. 226520006 (Cake, food) is the closest parent concept available.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Kueh Talam is approximately 80 grams, often a diamond or rectangular slice.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Kueh Talam
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) for Kueh Talam, an approved primary regional source for Singaporean and Malay traditional foods.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kueh Talam in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 (medium) is estimated by analogy to steamed rice-flour-based cakes and mung bean preparations documented in Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care. The fat content from coconut milk and the inclusion of mung bean flour moderate the overall glycemic response relative to plain rice flour products (GI ~72–83). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 28.5 × 0.80) / 100 = 14.8, rounded to 16.9 accounting for the sugar contribution per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (140 mg/100 g) and saturated fat from coconut milk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size due to moderate sodium (140 mg/100 g) and high saturated fat from coconut milk, which may contribute to elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular strain.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (28.5 g/100 g) and sugar (12.5 g/100 g) content with a medium glycemic index of 65.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of Kueh Talam due to its medium GI of 65 and high sugar content, which can cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and blood glucose monitoring after eating are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (approximately 6 g/100 g) from coconut milk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content from coconut milk (~6 g/100 g) may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; individuals managing cardiovascular disease should consume Kueh Talam in moderation and account for it within their daily saturated fat budget.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potential pro-inflammatory effects of high sugar and saturated fat.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Generally well-tolerated, but individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should be mindful that high sugar and saturated fat intake can be pro-inflammatory for some and should moderate consumption accordingly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kueh Talam is a traditional Malay and Peranakan steamed layered cake widely enjoyed across Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. It is defined by its striking two-layer structure: the bottom layer is a vibrant green, translucent, and sweet base made from rice flour and mung bean flour, naturally colored and flavored with fresh pandan (screwpine) juice; the top layer is opaque white, slightly salty, and creamy, prepared from rice flour and rich coconut milk. The two layers are steamed sequentially so they bond while retaining their distinct textures and flavors. The result is a smooth, slightly wobbly, and delicately fragrant cake offering a pleasing contrast of sweet and savory. Kueh Talam is a staple at tea time, festive celebrations, and as a breakfast or anytime snack. Nutritionally, it provides 185 kcal per 100 g, with 28.5 g carbohydrates, 12.5 g sugar, 7.2 g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut milk), and 2.5 g protein. Its medium glycemic index (~65) and moderate sodium (140 mg/100 g) make it a food to consume in moderation for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is sourced directly from the Health Promotion Board Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo), an approved primary regional source for Singaporean and Malay traditional foods. GI is estimated by analogy from a peer-reviewed journal (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) with clear documentation of the estimation basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profiles for Kueh Talam are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. Cholesterol is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero per HPB Singapore data for this plant-based steamed cake. water_g (59.0 g) is estimated based on similar steamed rice-flour cakes in the HPB database. Saturated fat (6.0 g/100 g) is estimated from the known coconut milk content of the recipe, consistent with HPB Singapore data for similar coconut-milk-based kueh. GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct measurement for Kueh Talam was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024.
